Package noppes.npcs.api.entity

Interface IEnergyProjectile<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IEnergyAbility<T>, IEntity<T>
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    IEnergyBeam<T>, IEnergyDisc<T>, IEnergyLaser<T>, IEnergyOrb<T>, IEnergySlicer<T>
    public interface IEnergyProjectile<T extends net.minecraft.entity.Entity>
extends IEnergyAbility<T>
    Base interface for all energy projectile entities (Orb, Beam, Disc, Laser, Slicer).

    Extends IEnergyAbility for shared display, lightning, owner, and charging methods.

    Fire methods — launch from current position (no repositioning):

    • fireAt(IEntity) — toward an entity
    • fireAt(double, double, double) — toward world coordinates
    • fireDirection(float, float) — in a yaw/pitch direction
    FireFrom methods — reposition to caster's eye, then launch (look-vector snap):
    • fireFrom(IEntityLivingBase) — fire along caster's look direction
    • fireFrom(IEntityLivingBase, IEntity) — fire toward a target entity

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void fireAt​(double x, double y, double z)
      Spawns the projectile and launches it toward the specified world coordinates.
      void fireAt​(IEntity target)
      Spawns the projectile and launches it from its current position toward the target entity.
      void fireDirection​(float yaw, float pitch)
      Spawns the projectile and launches it in the specified yaw/pitch direction.
      void fireFrom​(IEntityLivingBase caster)
      Sets the owner, positions the projectile at the caster's eye level, and launches it using the entity's full launch sequence (startMoving/startFiring).
      void fireFrom​(IEntityLivingBase caster, IEntity target)
      Sets the owner, positions the projectile at the caster's eye level, and launches it toward the target entity using the entity's full launch sequence.

    • Method Detail

      • setOwner

        void setOwner​(IEntity owner)
        Sets the owner entity (used for homing return, damage attribution, etc.).
        Parameters:
        owner - the entity that owns this projectile

        int getTargetEntityId()
        Returns:
        the entity ID of the current homing/tracking target, or -1 if none

      • getTarget

        IEntity getTarget()
        Returns:
        the current homing/tracking target entity, or null if none

      • setTarget

        void setTarget​(IEntity target)
        Sets the homing/tracking target entity. Pass null to clear.
        Parameters:
        target - the entity to home towards

        maximum travel distance before the projectile despawns (0 = unlimited)

        maximum lifetime in ticks before the projectile despawns (0 = unlimited)

        int getHitType()
        Returns:
        hit behavior type (0 = single hit, 1 = pierce, 2 = multi-hit with delay)

        void setHitType​(int hitType)

        boolean hasHit()
        Returns:
        true if the projectile has hit something

      • getEnergyType

        int getEnergyType()
        Returns the energy projectile sub-type.
        Returns:
        0=Orb, 1=Beam, 2=Disc, 3=Laser, 4=Slicer

      • syncClient

        void syncClient()
        Sends all current visual, movement, and position data to tracking clients.

        Call this after making batch changes to properties like size, speed, homing, colors, or type-specific fields (beam width, disc radius, etc.). This avoids sending a separate packet for every setter call.

        Note: fireAt(), fireDirection(), and fireFrom() automatically sync motion when called on an already-spawned projectile, so you do not need to call syncClient() after firing. Use this method when changing properties that affect rendering or movement behavior without re-firing.

        Example: 

         orb.setSize(2.0);
 orb.setSpeed(0.5);
 orb.setInnerColor(0xFF0000);
 orb.syncClient(); // one packet for all changes

      • fireAt

        void fireAt​(IEntity target)
        Spawns the projectile and launches it from its current position toward the target entity.

        No look-vector repositioning occurs — the projectile fires from exactly where it was placed by createOrb/createBeam/etc. If the target is a living entity, it is also set as the homing target for projectiles with homing enabled.

        To launch from the owner's eye position with look-vector snapping, use fireFrom(IEntityLivingBase, IEntity) instead.

        Parameters:
        target - the entity to fire toward (can be null for untargeted launch)

      • fireAt

        void fireAt​(double x,
            double y,
            double z)
        Spawns the projectile and launches it toward the specified world coordinates.

        The projectile fires from its current position — no look-vector repositioning occurs. Motion is calculated as a normalized direction vector scaled by the projectile's speed.

        Parameters:
        x - target X coordinate
        y - target Y coordinate
        z - target Z coordinate

      • fireDirection

        void fireDirection​(float yaw,
                   float pitch)
        Spawns the projectile and launches it in the specified yaw/pitch direction.

        The projectile fires from its current position — no look-vector repositioning occurs.

        Parameters:
        yaw - horizontal angle in degrees (0 = south, 90 = west, like Minecraft yaw)
        pitch - vertical angle in degrees (negative = up, positive = down)

      • fireFrom

        void fireFrom​(IEntityLivingBase caster)
        Sets the owner, positions the projectile at the caster's eye level, and launches it using the entity's full launch sequence (startMoving/startFiring).

        For player-owned projectiles, this snaps the projectile to the owner's look-vector position and fires in the caster's look direction. This is the intended method for "cast from self" behavior. The projectile's position set by createOrb/etc. is overridden.

        Use fireAt(IEntity) or fireAt(double, double, double) instead if you want to launch from a specific custom position.

        Parameters:
        caster - the entity to fire from

      • fireFrom

        void fireFrom​(IEntityLivingBase caster,
              IEntity target)
        Sets the owner, positions the projectile at the caster's eye level, and launches it toward the target entity using the entity's full launch sequence.

        For player-owned projectiles, this snaps the projectile to the owner's look-vector position before firing toward the target. The projectile's position set by createOrb/etc. is overridden.

        Use fireAt(IEntity) instead if you want to launch from a specific custom position.

        Parameters:
        caster - the entity to fire from
        target - the entity to fire toward
